subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The exploration is coherent and logically developed with clear narrative flow (rationale â modelling â cases â conclusion).
Headings, sub-headings, figures and tables give a well-organized structure, making information easy to locate.
Content is mostly relevant to the research question with only minor unnecessary elements.
weaknesses: Verbose step-by-step screenshots and some duplicate explanations inflate length and reduce conciseness.
Inconsistent citation formatting and incomplete bibliographic details interrupt the flow.
Table cell alignment and lengthy picture sequences sometimes break presentation coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments gave band scores of 3 for coherence, 3 for organization and 2 for conciseness. Taken together, the work is well organized and coherent but not exceptional in conciseness, so a holistic score of 3/4 accurately reflects its overall quality.

strengths: Notation for sets, edges, weights and algorithms is used mostly consistently and accurately.
Maps, weighted graphs, node-order diagrams and tables provide multiple, effective representations.
Arguments follow a clear logical structure; each algorithmic step is justified and intermediate results are tabulated.
weaknesses: Minor spelling slips (e.g. âEulieranâ) and informal arrow notation occur in a few places.
Some symbols (e.g. X vs. overline X) lack a legend, and map captions omit a scale bar.
Inconsistent numbering of algorithm steps slightly reduces clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands were assessed at the âgoodâ to âmostly consistentâ level. While there are minor lapses in spelling and legend definition, the overall communication is clear, structured and employs multiple effective representations, meriting 4/4.

strengths: The adaptation of the Chinese Postman Problem to a community trash-collection context shows significant independent thought.
Personal narrative around local clean-ups and bespoke map creation demonstrates a clear personal stamp.
Extension to two traffic-condition cases indicates good creativity and initiative.
weaknesses: The methodology follows standard graph-theoretic procedures rather than introducing novel mathematical ideas.
The personal approach, while clear, remains within expected IB exploration conventions.
Creativity is solid but does not reach highly innovative or exceptional levels.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each PE strand was assessed at band 2 (âsignificant evidenceâ, âclear personal approachâ, âgood creativityâ). The work shows genuine engagement but not outstanding innovation, so 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on duplicated visits, load-drop possibilities and alternative weightings appears in the conclusion.
Thoughtful analysis comparing total distances for both cases explains node duplication.
Clear evaluation of practical outcomes and suggestions for future improvements (e.g. incline, trash density).
weaknesses: Reflection lacks deep critical engagement with algorithmic limitations or computational complexity.
Quantitative reflection on performance and complexity is missing.
Evaluation is clear but not comprehensive in exploring broader implications.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All reflection strands were placed in band 2 (meaningful reflection, thoughtful analysis, clear evaluation). The work engages reflectively but does not sustain deep critical or future-oriented insight, warranting 2/3.

strengths: Graph theory concepts (Eulerian, semi-Eulerian) and algorithms (Edmonds, Fleury) are applied correctly and relevantly.
The level of mathematics is fully commensurate with the SL course and executed confidently.
Weight calculations, node pairings and final route computations are accurate with only minor typographical slips.
weaknesses: An explicit weight function w:Eââ and unit notation (metres) are not formally introduced.
Step 2 of Edmondsâ algorithm lacks an illustrative example on Graph 1.
The derivation of the factorial formula could be more fully justified in prose.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands were assessed at band 4 (relevance and level appropriateness) and one at band 5 (accuracy and understanding). The mathematics is thorough and correct with minor formal omissions, justifying 5/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are usually easy to follow; each algorithmic step is justified and intermediate results tabulated.  Some long narrative passages with screenshots reduce crispness, but overall communication retains good logical structure and clarity.

Calculations of weights, node orders, pairing combinations and final routes are accurate; algorithms are followed without substantive errors.  Small typographical slips do not detract from the mathematical correctness, demonstrating thorough understanding consistent with band 5.

Maps, weighted graphs, directed graphs, node-order diagrams, tables of pairings and algebraic expressions are combined effectively, allowing the reader to view the mathematics from several perspectives.  Whilst useful, they are not refined to the level of âhighly effectiveâ throughout.

The investigation follows a clear narrative: rationale, modelling, algorithms, two separate cases, results and conclusion.  Arguments build on each other in a logical order with only minor digressions; therefore the work is coherent though not of âexcellentâ seamless quality.

Employing graph theory for a community cycling problem and extending to a mixed-graph case indicates good creativity and initiative, though the mathematics itself stays close to textbook algorithms.  Thus the strand fits the âgoodâ level.

The student analyses results by comparing total distances for both cases and explaining why node duplication occurs.  Commentary is thoughtful yet not probing the mathematical limitations of algorithms, placing it in the âthoughtfulâ tier.

Outcomes are evaluated against practical aims and future improvements (e.g. incorporating incline or trash density).  Evaluation is clear but not comprehensive with quantitative support, hence band 2.
Reflection appears in the conclusion, discussing duplicated visits, load-drop possibilities and alternative weightings.  This constitutes meaningful but not deep critical reflection, matching band 2.

Correct notation for sets, edges, weights and algorithms is employed consistently in most sections.  Minor spelling slips (e.g. âEulieranâ) and the informal use of arrows in the typed text prevent the work from reaching the âconsistently accurateâ standard.

Choice of scenario, bespoke map creation and consideration of two different traffic conditions provide a clear personal stamp.  The approach is genuine but remains within standard methodology, so evidence is âclearâ rather than âhighly innovativeâ.

Most material directly serves the aim, but the many step-by-step screen-shots of graph deletion and several duplicate explanations inflate the length to 20 pages.  These repetitions reduce conciseness; nevertheless irrelevant content is limited, so âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ fits best.

Graph theory, Eulerian concepts, Edmondsâ and Fleuryâs algorithms are all relevant and applied correctly to solve the stated problem.  The mathematics is effective and well linked to the context, aligning with band 4.

The student adapts the Chinese Postman Problem to a personal trash-collection context and recognises the need for a semi-Eulerian rather than Eulerian solution, showing significant independent thought, yet the work primarily applies known procedures rather than devising new mathematics.

Headings, sub-headings, figures and tables give the exploration an easily followed structure (Introduction â Calculations â Cases â Conclusion).  The reader can locate information quickly, albeit some long picture sequences slightly break the flow so the organisation is judged âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionalâ.

The level of mathematics (non-syllabus graph theory for IB AA HL) is fully appropriate and executed with confidence.  It is not exceptionally advanced or original, so band 4 (âfully commensurateâ) fits best.

annotations.comment: The title precisely states the investigationâs objective, providing a clear focus on optimizing bike distance through the Chinese Postman Problem framework.
The personal narrative describing community clean-ups demonstrates genuine engagement and independence, effectively contextualizing the mathematical investigation.
The citation â(Garrett).â is too brief; include full bibliographic details or a consistent numbering style to ensure precise referencing.
Consider ending the introduction with a succinct, explicitly stated research question to guide coherence and signal the investigationâs focus.
The methodology description in âOutline of the studyâ is comprehensive but verbose. A concise bulleted summary would improve readability and eliminate repetition.
The conversion of map features into nodes, weighted edges, and directed edges is explained clearly, demonstrating strong mathematical communication.
Map 1 effectively provides a spatial representation, aiding multiple perspectives on the problem; this supports clarity in mathematical communication.
The map caption lacks a scale bar or indication of distance proportion. Including a scale would strengthen the precision of the weighted graph model.
Annotate the one-way road in the map legend with a clear arrow symbol to avoid any ambiguity in case 2âs directed graph construction.
The set notation $$L=(N,E)$$ with $$N=\{A,\dots,M\}$$ is precise and uses correct mathematical language.
Edge set \(E\) is listed without associating weights. Introduce a weight function \(w:E\to\mathbb{R}\) to formalize distances mathematically.
The informal citation â(âSeymourâs Secondâ)â interrupts flow. Use a numbered reference or more detailed authorâtitle format.
The directed edge set list contains duplications and unclear formatting. Streamline notation to avoid repetition (e.g., distinguish \(AB\) from \(BA\)).
Introduce unit notation in weight calculations (e.g., metres) and define a weight function \(w(e)\) to maintain dimensional clarity.
The explanation of Eulerian, semi-Eulerian, and non-Eulerian graphs is accurate, demonstrating thorough understanding of graph traversability.
Step 2 of Edmondsâ algorithm lists odd nodes but omits an explicit example. Demonstrate on Graph 1 to guide the reader.
Number algorithm steps consistently (e.g., align indentation) and refer back to them by number for clearer mathematical communication.
The summation $$30+27+32+\dots+27=608$$ is calculated correctly and demonstrates accurate application of weight addition.
Table 1 clearly summarises the factorial method for counting odd-node pairings, effectively supporting multiple representations.
The tableâs cell alignment appears inconsistent. Ensure uniform column widths and text alignment to improve presentation coherence.
Elaborate briefly on the derivation of the formula $$(n-1)\times(n-2)\times\cdots \times1$$ to strengthen the mathematical justification.
Symbols X and $\overline X$ in Table 2 lack a legend. Define these markers to avoid ambiguity in interpreting pair viability.
Before presenting dense tables, summarise key numerical findings in prose to guide the reader and maintain logical structure.
The highlighted minimised pairing in Table 4 demonstrates clear logical progression and effective use of tabular data.
In the conclusion section, include quantitative reflection on algorithmic limitations (e.g., computational complexity) to deepen critical analysis.
